title: Homepage day-header: hoist Intl formatters + tomorrowIso, type coordinates
detail:
[1] Homepage day-header: hoist Intl formatters + tomorrowIso, type coordinates
      FILES: frontend/src/routes/+page.svelte
      Three findings on this one file (cluster into one PR): (1) The `dayHeader` snippet (lines 200-201) constructs two `new Intl.DateTimeFormat('en-GB', {...timeZone:'Europe/London'})` inline on every invocation (rendered up to MAX_DAYS_VISIBLE=7 times per render and on every filter change). Hoist both to module-scope consts (WEEKDAY_FMT with {weekday:'long'}, DAY_NUM_FMT with {day:'numeric'}) and call `.format(d)`. (2) Line 203 runs an IIFE recomputing `tomorrowIso` per day-header render even though it depends only on the constant `todayStore.value`; replace with a single component-scope `const tomorrowIso = $derived(...)` keyed off todayStore.value and reference it in the snippet. (3) The `cinemas` $derived cast (lines 34-39) omits `coordinates`, but DesktopFilterSidebar (lines 50-51) and MobileFilterSheet (lines 58-59) read `c.coordinates`/`c.coordinates!` for haversine radius filtering; add `coordinates: { lat: number; lng: number } | null` to the cast type so the contract is checked at the boundary. All three are behavior-preserving; output byte-identical.

title: parse-query: memoize phrase Sets/maxLen + hoist constant arrays off keystroke hot path
detail:
[1] parse-query: memoize phrase Sets/maxLen + hoist constant arrays off keystroke hot path
      FILES: frontend/src/lib/search/parse-query.ts
      Four findings on this one file (cluster into one PR). parseQuery runs via a $derived on every cmd+k keystroke (palette.svelte.ts:84), and calls scanPhrases ~9x per keystroke over constant module-level tables. (1) scanPhrases (line 196) does `new Set(phrases)` inside the len loop on every call — memoize per phrase-array (module-level WeakMap<string[],Set<string>> or precompute a Set alongside each *_BY_LENGTH table). (2) Same function line 192 recomputes `Math.max(0, ...Object.keys(phrasesByLength).map(Number))` per call over fixed data — fold maxLen into the same per-table memoization. (3) Line 377 builds `Object.keys(DAY_NAMES).map((d) => `next ${d}`)` (14-element array) inline on every parseQuery call — hoist to a module-level `const NEXT_DAY_PHRASES_BY_LENGTH = { 2: ... }`. (4) applyNextDay (line 277) and applyDayThisWeek (line 292) each allocate an identical `["SUN","MON","TUE","WED","THU","FRI","SAT"]` literal — lift to a single module-level `const WEEKDAY_LABELS` and index in both. All produce byte-identical parse output (pure constant data).

title: DayMasthead: reuse formatOrdinalDay helper + hoist three Intl formatters (one in a loop)
detail:
[1] DayMasthead: reuse formatOrdinalDay helper + hoist three Intl formatters (one in a loop)
      FILES: frontend/src/lib/components/calendar/DayMasthead.svelte
      Two findings on this one file (cluster into one PR). (1) Lines 17-25 declare a local 31-entry `const ORDINALS` and line 33 derives `ORDINALS[dayNum] ?? `${dayNum}th``. `$lib/utils` already exports `formatOrdinalDay(dayNum)` (utils.ts:195, backed by ORDINAL_DAYS with the identical `?? `${n}th`` fallback, already used by the homepage day-header). Import `formatOrdinalDay` and replace with `const ordinal = $derived(formatOrdinalDay(dayNum))`, deleting the local ORDINALS record. (2) Three Intl.DateTimeFormat are constructed on every recompute: `weekday` derived (line 28, {weekday:'long'}), `dayNum` derived (line 31, {day:'numeric'}), and one inside stripItems' $derived.by loop (line 51, {weekday:'short'}) which allocates 4 per recompute. Hoist all three to module-scope consts (WEEKDAY_LONG, DAY_NUM, WEEKDAY_SHORT) matching the utils.ts:5-21 cached-formatter pattern. Behavior-preserving; same locale/options.

title: BreathingGrid: read getBoundingClientRect once per frame instead of per cell
detail:
[1] BreathingGrid: read getBoundingClientRect once per frame instead of per cell
      FILES: frontend/src/lib/components/pretext/BreathingGrid.svelte
      getCharScale is invoked once per character cell in `{#each chars as cell}` (line 106). When the pointer is over the header (`mx > -500`), each call performs `containerEl.getBoundingClientRect()` (line 41) — a layout-forcing read — so with ~14 cells at 60fps that is ~14 forced reflows per frame during hover, all returning the identical rect for the frame. Read the container rect once per frame when mouse-active (compute it before the loop / pass it into getCharScale) and reuse for all cells. All cells in a single synchronous render observe the same layout so scale values are byte-identical; cuts forced-reflow reads from O(cells) to O(1) per frame on the always-mounted header logo animation.

title: Cinemas index: drop dead groupBy import + hoist search.toLowerCase out of filter
detail:
[1] Cinemas index: drop dead groupBy import + hoist search.toLowerCase out of filter
      FILES: frontend/src/routes/cinemas/+page.svelte
      Two findings on this one file (cluster into one PR). (1) `import { groupBy } from '$lib/utils'` (line 3) is dead — the `grouped` $derived (lines 17-28) reimplements the grouping inline and never calls groupBy; grep confirms the symbol appears only on the import line. Remove the import. (2) In the `filtered` $derived (lines 8-15) `search.toLowerCase()` is recomputed twice per cinema (~118 redundant lowercasings per keystroke across ~59 cinemas). Compute `const q = search.toLowerCase();` once before the `.filter` and use `q` in both `c.name.toLowerCase().includes(q)` and `(c.address?.area.toLowerCase().includes(q) ?? false)`. Identical results.

title: Film detail page: drop dead formatScreeningDate import + remove redundant re-sort
detail:
[1] Film detail page: drop dead formatScreeningDate import + remove redundant re-sort
      FILES: frontend/src/routes/film/[id]/+page.svelte
      Two findings on this one file (cluster into one PR). (1) The named import `formatScreeningDate,` (line 11, inside the `$lib/utils` import block) is never referenced — the page builds day labels via its own helpers; grep shows the token appears only on the import line. Remove just that entry, keeping the other imports. (2) `groupedByDate` (lines 103-106) does `Object.entries(groupBy(futureScreenings, toLondonDateStr)).sort(([a],[b]) => a.localeCompare(b))`, but futureScreenings (lines 69-80) is already sorted ascending by epoch-ms; since toLondonDateStr is monotonic in ms and JS preserves string-key insertion order, entries are already chronological and the localeCompare sort is a provable no-op. Replace with `return Object.entries(grouped);`. Both behavior-preserving.

title: Cinema detail server load: trim unused screening fields from SSR payload
detail:
[1] Cinema detail server load: trim unused screening fields from SSR payload
      FILES: frontend/src/routes/cinemas/[slug]/+page.server.ts
      The load (lines 60-75) maps each screening to `{ id, datetime, format, bookingUrl, screen, film: { id, title, year, directors, runtime, posterUrl } }`, but the [slug]/+page.svelte only reads datetime, format, bookingUrl, film.id, film.title, film.year (the `.screen-count` CSS renders the unrelated `cinema.screens`, not `screening.screen`; grep confirms zero refs to screening `screen`, `film.directors`, `film.runtime`, `film.posterUrl` in the component). Drop `screen` plus `directors`/`runtime`/`posterUrl` from the per-screening map. Cinema pages list dozens-to-hundreds of screenings, so this removes real dead weight (directors string[] + posterUrl per screening) from the serialized __data/hydration payload with no rendered-output change. Distinct from the already-shipped search_text/calendar trims.

title: Reachable server load: drop unused director + isRepertory from screening payload
detail:
[1] Reachable server load: drop unused director + isRepertory from screening payload
      FILES: frontend/src/routes/reachable/+page.server.ts
      The load maps each screening's film with `director: s.film.directors?.[0] ?? null` (line 62) and `isRepertory: s.film.isRepertory` (line 65), but reachable/+page.svelte re-maps data.screenings reading only film.{id,title,year,runtime,posterUrl} (grep confirms zero `director`/`isRepertory` consumers in the component). Remove both fields from the returned mapping. Trims two dead fields per screening (up to ~200 screenings) from the serialized page data; rendered output is byte-identical.

title: Search server load: drop unused shortName from cinema payload
detail:
[1] Search server load: drop unused shortName from cinema payload
      FILES: frontend/src/routes/search/+page.server.ts
      load returns each cinema with `shortName: c.shortName` (line 43), but search/+page.svelte renders only `cinema.name` (line 86) and `cinema.area` (line 88) — grep finds no shortName usage. Drop the `shortName` field from the returned cinema objects. Shrinks the SSR payload with no change to rendered output.

title: Homepage server load: drop unused meta payload
detail:
[1] Homepage server load: drop unused meta payload
      FILES: frontend/src/routes/+page.server.ts
      load returns `meta: { total, startDate, endDate }` (lines 73-77), but a repo-wide grep shows the only references to `.meta.total/startDate/endDate` are the lines building it here — +page.svelte and client components never read `data.meta`. (Note: film/[id]/+page.server.ts has its own separate `meta: data.meta` return, which is a different file and out of scope here.) Remove the `meta` key from the homepage returned object (optionally drop the now-unused `meta` field from the ScreeningsResponse interface if not needed for fetch typing). Trims dead data from the SSR/ISR payload on the LCP path with zero consumer.

title: Festival detail: drop dead type import + decorate-sort the per-group screenings
detail:
[1] Festival detail: drop dead type import + decorate-sort the per-group screenings
      FILES: frontend/src/routes/festivals/[slug]/+page.svelte
      Two findings on this one file (cluster into one PR). (1) `import type { ScreeningWithDetails } from '$lib/types'` (line 5) is never referenced (grep confirms single occurrence) — delete the line (type-only, erased at compile time). (2) In the `filmGroups` $derived.by (lines 12-18) each group sorts with `ss.sort((a,b) => new Date(a.datetime).getTime() - new Date(b.datetime).getTime())`, allocating two Date objects per comparator call (O(n log n) parses). Decorate each screening once with `_ms = new Date(s.datetime).getTime()` (mirroring the established pattern in cinemas/[slug] and film/[id]) and compare `a._ms - b._ms`. Identical sort order.

title: Watchlist: drop dead Badge import, narrow sortBy union, hoist now out of filter
detail:
[1] Watchlist: drop dead Badge import, narrow sortBy union, hoist now out of filter
      FILES: frontend/src/routes/watchlist/+page.svelte
      Three findings on this one file (cluster into one PR). (1) `import Badge from '$lib/components/ui/Badge.svelte'` (line 5) is unused — grep confirms only the import line; remove it. (2) `let sortBy = $state<'next' | 'added' | 'title'>('next')` (line 22) — `'added'` is never assigned (only NEXT and A–Z buttons set 'next'/'title' at lines 106-107) and sortedFilms (lines 69-82) has no 'added' branch; narrow the type to `'next' | 'title'`. (3) In loadFilms, `res.screenings.filter((s) => new Date(s.datetime) > new Date())` (line 43) allocates a fresh `new Date()` per screening; compute `const now = Date.now()` once before the filter and compare `new Date(s.datetime).getTime() > now`. All behavior-preserving.

title: Cinema detail page: remove redundant re-sort of already-ascending date groups
detail:
[1] Cinema detail page: remove redundant re-sort of already-ascending date groups
      FILES: frontend/src/routes/cinemas/[slug]/+page.svelte
      `groupedByDate` (lines 31-34) does `Object.entries(groupBy(futureScreenings, ...)).sort(([a],[b]) => a.localeCompare(b))`, but futureScreenings is already sorted strictly ascending by epoch-ms just above (lines 16-29). toLondonDateStr is monotonic non-decreasing in ms and JS preserves string-key insertion order, so the groups are already chronological and the localeCompare sort is a guaranteed no-op. Replace with `return Object.entries(grouped);`. Removes an O(k log k) comparator + closure per re-derivation; output order identical. (Separate PR from the [slug] server-load trim — different file.)

title: Map page: drop dead page import + remove any cast on venue-count filter
detail:
[1] Map page: drop dead page import + remove any cast on venue-count filter
      FILES: frontend/src/routes/map/+page.svelte
      Two findings on this one file (cluster into one PR). (1) `import { page } from '$app/state'` (line 3) is unused — the only `page` matches are CSS class `.map-page` and `aria-labelledby`; remove the import to drop $app/state from the page graph. (2) Line 20: `cinemas.filter((c: any) => c.coordinates)` — `cinemas` is `$derived(data?.cinemas ?? [])` typed Cinema[] (Cinema has `coordinates: CinemaCoordinates | null`); remove `: any` so `c` infers as Cinema and the count expression stays type-checked. Zero runtime change.

title: CinemaMap: remove dead validCinemas + hoist marker/popup literal constants
detail:
[1] CinemaMap: remove dead validCinemas + hoist marker/popup literal constants
      FILES: frontend/src/lib/components/map/CinemaMap.svelte
      Three findings on this one file (cluster into one PR). (1) `const validCinemas = $derived(cinemas.filter(...))` (lines 10-14) is never read (the onMount recomputes the identical filter inline as `cinemasWithCoords`, lines 97-100); grep confirms validCinemas has no other reference in the component. Delete the dead $derived block. (2) createMarkerElement (lines 38-71) is called per cinema and rebuilds identical literal strings each call — SVG width/height/viewBox ('20'/'26'/'0 0 20 26'), path d ('M10 0C4.5 0...Z'), fills ('#1a1a1a'/'#fff'), circle cx/cy/r; hoist these to module-scope consts. (3) createPopupContent (lines 16-36) rebuilds constant cssText strings each call (link.style.cssText line 23, area.style.cssText line 31, div padding '4px'); promote to module-scope string consts. Identical DOM output.

title: ReachableResults: compute urgencyClass once per group via {@const}
DeadlinePicker: reuse cached Intl.DateTimeFormat instances in formatSelectedTime
detail:
[1] DeadlinePicker: reuse cached Intl.DateTimeFormat instances in formatSelectedTime
      FILES: frontend/src/lib/components/reachable/DeadlinePicker.svelte
      formatSelectedTime (lines 64-77) makes up to five toLocaleDateString/toLocaleTimeString calls per invocation, each constructing a fresh formatter from inline options: three `toLocaleDateString('en-CA', { timeZone:'Europe/London' })` (lines 66/69/70), one `toLocaleTimeString('en-GB', { hour:'2-digit', minute:'2-digit', hour12:false })` (line 72), one `toLocaleDateString('en-GB', { weekday:'short', day:'numeric', month:'short' })` (line 76). Hoist three module-scope `Intl.DateTimeFormat` consts ('en-CA' London-tz YMD; 'en-GB' HH:mm; 'en-GB' 'Mon, 1 Jan') and call `.format(d)` on the reused instances. Matches the established hoist pattern (ScreeningRow). Same YYYY-MM-DD / HH:mm / 'Mon, 1 Jan' strings.

title: FestivalRow: hoist date-range Intl.DateTimeFormat to script module scope
detail:
[1] FestivalRow: hoist date-range Intl.DateTimeFormat to script module scope
      FILES: frontend/src/lib/components/search/rows/FestivalRow.svelte
      The `dateRange` $derived.by (lines 21-28) constructs `new Intl.DateTimeFormat('en-GB', { month:'short', day:'numeric', timeZone:'Europe/London' })` on every recompute, once per festival row. The args are fully constant. Move it to a `<script module>` block as a shared `const RANGE_FORMATTER = new Intl.DateTimeFormat(...)` — exactly the pattern the sibling ScreeningRow.svelte already uses (`<script module>` with TIME_FORMATTER/WEEKDAY_FORMATTER/LONDON_DATE_FORMATTER, confirmed) — and have dateRange reference it. Byte-identical output.

title: Header: precompute NAV_ITEMS desktop/mobile filters once
detail:
[1] Header: precompute NAV_ITEMS desktop/mobile filters once
      FILES: frontend/src/lib/components/layout/Header.svelte
      The desktop and mobile nav lists are filtered inline in markup: `{#each NAV_ITEMS.filter((n) => n.desktop) ...}` (line 85) and `{#each NAV_ITEMS.filter((n) => n.mobile) ...}` (line 128). NAV_ITEMS is a static const (lines 33-41) never mutated, yet both .filter() passes re-run on every re-render of the sticky header (every page.url.pathname change, every mobileMenuOpen toggle). Hoist to two script-scope consts computed once: `const DESKTOP_NAV = NAV_ITEMS.filter((n) => n.desktop);` and `const MOBILE_NAV = NAV_ITEMS.filter((n) => n.mobile);`, then iterate those (keys `(item.href)` unchanged). Byte-identical render.

title: MobileFilterSheet: hoist inline Date getUTCDate() for Today/Tomorrow chips
detail:
[1] MobileFilterSheet: hoist inline Date getUTCDate() for Today/Tomorrow chips
      FILES: frontend/src/lib/components/filters/MobileFilterSheet.svelte
      Lines 200-201 render `{new Date(today + 'T12:00:00Z').getUTCDate()}` and `{new Date(tomorrow + 'T12:00:00Z').getUTCDate()}` inline, allocating two Date objects on every re-render of the open sheet. `today`/`tomorrow` are already instance consts (lines 82-87) and todayLabel/tomorrowLabel are precomputed the same way (lines 89-90). Add `const todayDay = new Date(today + 'T12:00:00Z').getUTCDate();` and `const tomorrowDay = new Date(tomorrow + 'T12:00:00Z').getUTCDate();` next to the labels and reference `{todayDay}`/`{tomorrowDay}`. Day numbers are fixed for the component lifetime; byte-identical output.

title: filters store: hoist duplicated en-CA London Intl formatter out of applyIntent
detail:
[1] filters store: hoist duplicated en-CA London Intl formatter out of applyIntent
      FILES: frontend/src/lib/stores/filters.svelte.ts
      In applyIntent (lines 271-286) two byte-identical `new Intl.DateTimeFormat('en-CA', { timeZone:'Europe/London', year:'numeric', month:'2-digit', day:'2-digit' })` instances are constructed inline — one for dateFrom (lines 272-277), one for dateTo (lines 280-285) — on every call. Hoist a single module-level `const LONDON_YMD = new Intl.DateTimeFormat('en-CA', {...})` near the top and call `LONDON_YMD.format(parsed.dateFrom)` / `LONDON_YMD.format(parsed.dateTo)`. Reused formatter is stateless and yields identical YYYY-MM-DD across DST; removes 2 allocations per applyIntent call. Mirrors the established hoist pattern (DATE_LONDON_ISO in utils.ts).

title: CalendarPopover: hoist inline weekday-header array to a module const
detail:
[1] CalendarPopover: hoist inline weekday-header array to a module const
      FILES: frontend/src/lib/components/filters/CalendarPopover.svelte
      Line 96 iterates `{#each ['Mo','Tu','We','Th','Fr','Sa','Su'] as d, i (d + i)}` with the array literal inlined, allocating a fresh 7-element array on every render (e.g. each month nav click). Hoist to a top-level `const WEEKDAYS = ['Mo','Tu','We','Th','Fr','Sa','Su'];` alongside the existing MONTH_NAMES const (line 27) and iterate `{#each WEEKDAYS as d, i (d + i)}`. Same data, identical keys/output; matches the file's own MONTH_NAMES convention.

title: MobileDatePicker: hoist inline weekday-header array to a module const
detail:
[1] MobileDatePicker: hoist inline weekday-header array to a module const
      FILES: frontend/src/lib/components/filters/MobileDatePicker.svelte
      Line 88 iterates `{#each ['Mo','Tu','We','Th','Fr','Sa','Su'] as d, i (d + i)}` with the array literal inlined, re-allocating a 7-element array on every render (month nav). Hoist to a top-level `const WEEKDAYS = [...]` next to the existing MONTH_NAMES const (line 18) and iterate `{#each WEEKDAYS as d, i (d + i)}`. Constant data, identical keys/output; consistent with the file's MONTH_NAMES const.

title: Directors page: hoist query lowercasing out of the filter loop
detail:
[1] Directors page: hoist query lowercasing out of the filter loop
      FILES: frontend/src/routes/directors/+page.svelte
      Line 15: `data.directors.filter((d) => d.name.toLowerCase().includes(search.toLowerCase()))` recomputes `search.toLowerCase()` once per director on every keystroke. The directors API has no LIMIT (returns every director across the next 14 days — realistically several hundred entries), so compute the lowercased query once inside the $derived: `const q = search.toLowerCase();` then `.filter((d) => d.name.toLowerCase().includes(q))`. Identical output.

title: FilmRow: compute tmdbRating.toFixed(1) once
detail:
[1] FilmRow: compute tmdbRating.toFixed(1) once
      FILES: frontend/src/lib/components/search/rows/FilmRow.svelte
      `film.tmdbRating.toFixed(1)` is computed twice — in `aria-label="TMDB rating {film.tmdbRating.toFixed(1)}"` (line 45) and the visible `★ {film.tmdbRating.toFixed(1)}` (line 46). Compute once, e.g. `const rating = $derived(film.tmdbRating != null ? film.tmdbRating.toFixed(1) : null)`, gate the block on `rating != null && film.tmdbRating >= 7`, and reuse `rating` in both places. Rendered text/aria string identical; label and value can never drift.

title: LetterboxdRatingReveal: drop unused loop index in star #each blocks
detail:
[1] LetterboxdRatingReveal: drop unused loop index in star #each blocks
      FILES: frontend/src/lib/components/film/LetterboxdRatingReveal.svelte
      Both star loops bind an index never used: `{#each Array(displayStars) as _, i}` (line 34) and `{#each Array(Math.max(0, emptyStars)) as _, i}` (line 50) — `i` appears nowhere in either block body. Remove the `, i` to leave `{#each Array(displayStars) as _}` and `{#each Array(Math.max(0, emptyStars)) as _}`. Identical rendered SVG stars; stops the compiler tracking a dead per-iteration binding.

title: Root layout: extract duplicated app-shell into a {#snippet}
utils: drop redundant nested typeof guard in formatScreeningDate
detail:
[1] utils: drop redundant nested typeof guard in formatScreeningDate
      FILES: frontend/src/lib/utils.ts
      Line 52: `new Date(date + (typeof date === 'string' && !date.includes('T') ? 'T00:00:00' : ''))` sits inside the `typeof date === 'string' ? ... : date` branch where `date` is already narrowed to string, so the inner `typeof date === 'string' &&` is provably always true. Simplify to `new Date(date + (!date.includes('T') ? 'T00:00:00' : ''))`. Concatenation result byte-identical; clarifies control flow. (Single, low-risk edit on this widely-imported module — keep the PR scoped to just this line.)

title: result-types: delete unused totalResults export
detail:
[1] result-types: delete unused totalResults export
      FILES: frontend/src/lib/search/result-types.ts
      `export function totalResults(results: PaletteResults): number` (lines 172-174) has zero importers — grep across src finds only its own definition; the only other `totalResults` matches are an unrelated LOCAL `const totalResults` inside SearchInput.svelte (a different symbol). The palette store uses `flattenResults(...).length`/`flatRows.length` directly. Delete the export. NOTE: the separately-proposed 'optimize totalResults to avoid array materialization' finding is intentionally DROPPED — it targets the same function this PR deletes, so it is moot.

title: chains vocab: remove unused empty CHAIN_PHRASES_BY_LENGTH placeholder
detail:
[1] chains vocab: remove unused empty CHAIN_PHRASES_BY_LENGTH placeholder
      FILES: frontend/src/lib/search/vocab/chains.ts
      `export const CHAIN_PHRASES_BY_LENGTH: Record<number, string[]> = { 2: [] }` (lines 24-26) is an empty placeholder never imported — grep matches only its own definition. parse-query.ts imports CHAIN_TOKENS, CINEMA_ALIAS_TOKENS, CINEMA_ALIAS_PHRASES_BY_LENGTH from this file but NOT CHAIN_PHRASES_BY_LENGTH, and runs no chain-phrase scan. Delete the export. Removes a dead always-empty export implying a feature that does not exist.

title: postcode: remove dead getLocationName export
detail:
[1] postcode: remove dead getLocationName export
      FILES: frontend/src/lib/postcode.ts
      `export function getLocationName(result: PostcodeResult): string` (lines 104-109) has zero references repo-wide — grep confirms the symbol appears only at its own definition. The only postcode-coordinate consumer (PostcodeInput.svelte) inlines `result.admin_district || formatPostcode(...)` rather than calling this helper. Delete the ~6-line function. Public surface shrinks; behavior unchanged.

title: Festivals index: remove unused Badge import
detail:
[1] Festivals index: remove unused Badge import
      FILES: frontend/src/routes/festivals/+page.svelte
      Line 2 imports `Badge from '$lib/components/ui/Badge.svelte'` but a full-file grep finds zero references in template or script — only the import line. Delete the import. Svelte component imports are side-effect-free so runtime is unchanged; drops Badge from the page chunk.

title: Tonight server load: drop unused dateLabel field
detail:
[1] Tonight server load: drop unused dateLabel field
      FILES: frontend/src/routes/tonight/+page.server.ts
      Line 83 returns `dateLabel: 'TONIGHT'`, but tonight/+page.svelte never reads data.dateLabel (it computes its own todayLabel via formatDate) and a repo-wide grep finds no consumer of this load's dateLabel (the only other dateLabel reference is an unrelated local derived in DaySection.svelte). Remove the field. Trims a dead value from the SSR/ISR payload; rendered output unchanged.
